free_text: p-wave resonances observed by the (p,n) reaction for
2.6- to 7-MeV protons incident on isotopes of Sn
8 BF3 detectors placed within an 1.5 m graphite
sphere giving 4pi geometry. The absolute efficiency
was +-0.6% at an average energy of 500 keV .
The detector response was constant to better than
1% from a few keV to 1 MeV and to 7% from 1 to 5 MeV.
The neutrons detected have mainly energies below 1 MeV.
Detector calibrated with a National Bureau of Standards
Proton energy calibrated using the 19F(p,n) threshold
. room background and background due to target

For proton energies above 4 MeV, the systematic and
random uncertainties combine in quadrature to give
<1%.  Below 4 MeV, the uncertainties are larger
because of the decreasing ratio of yield to
